questionHtml:
Which solutions will form a buffer when mixed?


A.  50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 HCl and 50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 NaOH

B.  50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 CH3COOH and 50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 NaOH

C.  50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 CH3COOH and 100 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 NaOH

D.  100 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 CH3COOH and 50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 NaOH

meta.title: Which solutions will form a buffer when mixed? A. 50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 HCl and 50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 NaOH B. 50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 CH3COOH and 50 cm3 of 1.0 mol dm−3 NaOH C. 50
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.1.16—A buffer solution is one that resists change in pH on the addition of small amounts of acid or alkali. Describe the composition of acidic and basic buffers and explain their actions.
